name: Anadama Raisin Batter Bread
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Corn meal, preferably yellow and stone-ground
recipeInstructions:
Makes 2 loaves.
1. In a good-sized saucepan, bring the milk and 1 cup of water to a boil. Turn off the heat and sprinkle in the corn meal, stirring energetically with a wire whisk or fork. Add the butter and molasses and stir until evenly mixed. Cool to lukewarm.
2. In a large mixing bowl, dissolve the yeast in the half-cup warm water with the sugar or honey. When bubbly, add the cornmeal mixture, salt, and 2 1/2 cups of flour. It should be thick but still beatable.
3. Beat for 5 minutes with an electric mixer.
4. Stir in the raisins. Gradually add 2 to 3 cups more flour, beating with a large spoon until the batter is too stiff to continue beating and holds together in a mass in the center of the bowl.
5. Cover the bowl with a damp towel and let it sit until the dough has risen to twice its original volume. Stir down with the spoon. If desired, let it rise a second time. Stir down.
6. Divide the dough between two buttered large loaf pans. Make sure you get equal amounts in each pan. Press into the corners with your fingers and smooth the tops slightly; don't worry if they look like a lava flow.
7. Cover the pans with a towel and let rise until the batter reaches the tops of the pans. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
8. Bake for 40-45 minutes, or until the loaves are brown but do not have a hollow sound when tapped on the bottoms. Remove from the pans and place directly on the oven rack. Bake another 5 minutes to crisp the crust.
9. Cool on a rack.
